sections.heading: Population Genetics (Hardy-Weinberg)
sections.content:
**Allele frequencies:**

$$p + q = 1$$

**Genotype frequencies:**

$$p^2 + 2pq + q^2 = 1$$

$p$ = frequency of dominant allele, $q$ = frequency of recessive allele, $p^2$ = homozygous dominant, $2pq$ = heterozygous, $q^2$ = homozygous recessive

sections.heading: Water Potential
sections.content:
$$\psi = \psi_s + \psi_p$$

$\psi$ = water potential, $\psi_s$ = solute (osmotic) potential, $\psi_p$ = pressure potential

**Solute potential:**

$$\psi_s = -iCRT$$

$i$ = ionization constant, $C$ = molar concentration, $R$ = pressure constant (0.0831 L·bar/mol·K), $T$ = temperature (K)

sections.heading: Chi-Square Test
sections.content:
$$\chi^2 = \sum \frac{(o - e)^2}{e}$$

$o$ = observed value, $e$ = expected value

Degrees of freedom = number of categories $- 1$

sections.heading: Population Dynamics
sections.content:
**Exponential growth:**

$$\frac{dN}{dt} = rN$$

**Logistic growth:**

$$\frac{dN}{dt} = rN\left(\frac{K - N}{K}\right)$$

$N$ = population size, $r$ = per capita growth rate, $K$ = carrying capacity

**Net reproductive rate:** $R_0 = \sum l_x m_x$ where $l_x$ = survivorship, $m_x$ = fecundity

sections.heading: Surface Area & Volume
sections.content:
**Sphere:** $SA = 4\pi r^2$, $V = \frac{4}{3}\pi r^3$

**Cylinder:** $SA = 2\pi r^2 + 2\pi rh$, $V = \pi r^2 h$

SA:V ratio decreases as size increases — limits cell size.

sections.heading: Gas Laws
sections.content:
**Ideal Gas Law:**

$$PV = nRT$$

$R = 8.314\text{ J/mol·K} = 0.08206\text{ L·atm/mol·K}$

**Boyle's Law:** $P_1V_1 = P_2V_2$

**Charles's Law:** $\displaystyle\frac{V_1}{T_1} = \frac{V_2}{T_2}$

**Combined:** $\displaystyle\frac{P_1V_1}{T_1} = \frac{P_2V_2}{T_2}$

**Dalton's Law:** $P_{total} = P_1 + P_2 + \cdots$

sections.heading: Thermodynamics
sections.content:
$$\Delta G^\circ = \Delta H^\circ - T\Delta S^\circ$$

$$\Delta G^\circ = -RT\ln K$$

$$\Delta G = \Delta G^\circ + RT\ln Q$$

**Hess's Law:** $\Delta H_{rxn} = \sum \Delta H_f^\circ(\text{products}) - \sum \Delta H_f^\circ(\text{reactants})$

sections.heading: Equilibrium & Acids/Bases
sections.content:
**Equilibrium constant:** $K_c = \dfrac{[\text{products}]^x}{[\text{reactants}]^y}$

**Reaction quotient:** $Q = \dfrac{[\text{products}]^x}{[\text{reactants}]^y}$

**Water:** $K_w = [H^+][OH^-] = 1.0 \times 10^{-14}$ at 25°C

$$\text{pH} = -\log[H^+] \qquad \text{pOH} = -\log[OH^-]$$

$$\text{pH} + \text{pOH} = 14$$

**Henderson–Hasselbalch:**

$$\text{pH} = \text{p}K_a + \log\frac{[A^-]}{[HA]}$$

sections.heading: Electrochemistry
sections.content:
$$\Delta G^\circ = -nFE^\circ$$

**Nernst Equation:**

$$E = E^\circ - \frac{RT}{nF}\ln Q$$

At 25°C: $E = E^\circ - \dfrac{0.0592}{n}\log Q$

$F = 96485$ C/mol, $n$ = moles of electrons transferred

sections.heading: Kinetics
sections.content:
**Rate law:** $\text{rate} = k[A]^m[B]^n$

**First-order integrated:** $\ln[A]_t = -kt + \ln[A]_0$

**Half-life (1st order):** $t_{1/2} = \dfrac{0.693}{k}$

**Second-order integrated:** $\dfrac{1}{[A]_t} = kt + \dfrac{1}{[A]_0}$

**Arrhenius:** $k = Ae^{-E_a/RT}$

sections.heading: Colligative Properties
sections.content:
**Molarity:** $M = \dfrac{\text{mol solute}}{\text{L solution}}$

**Molality:** $m = \dfrac{\text{mol solute}}{\text{kg solvent}}$

**Boiling point elevation:** $\Delta T_b = K_b \cdot m \cdot i$

**Freezing point depression:** $\Delta T_f = K_f \cdot m \cdot i$

sections.heading: Kinematics
sections.content:
$$v = v_0 + at$$

$$x = x_0 + v_0 t + \tfrac{1}{2}at^2$$

$$v^2 = v_0^2 + 2a\Delta x$$

$$\bar{v} = \frac{\Delta x}{\Delta t} \qquad \bar{a} = \frac{\Delta v}{\Delta t}$$

**Projectile (no air resistance):** horizontal $a = 0$, vertical $a = -g = -9.8$ m/s²

sections.heading: Newton's Laws & Forces
sections.content:
$$\sum \vec{F} = m\vec{a}$$

**Friction:** $f \leq \mu_s N \quad$ (static); $\quad f_k = \mu_k N$ (kinetic)

**Gravitational force:** $F_g = mg$ (near Earth's surface)

**Universal gravitation:** $F_g = \dfrac{Gm_1m_2}{r^2}$, \quad $G = 6.67 \times 10^{-11}$ N·m²/kg²

**Centripetal acceleration:** $a_c = \dfrac{v^2}{r}$

sections.heading: Work, Energy & Power
sections.content:
$$W = F d\cos\theta$$

$$KE = \tfrac{1}{2}mv^2 \qquad PE_g = mgh$$

**Spring PE:** $PE_s = \tfrac{1}{2}kx^2$ \quad (Hooke: $F_s = -kx$)

**Work-Energy Theorem:** $W_{net} = \Delta KE$

**Power:** $P = \dfrac{W}{\Delta t} = Fv$

sections.heading: Momentum & Impulse
sections.content:
$$\vec{p} = m\vec{v}$$

**Impulse:** $\vec{J} = \vec{F}\Delta t = \Delta\vec{p}$

**Conservation of momentum:** $\sum p_i = \sum p_f$ (no net external force)

**Elastic collision:** KE conserved; **Inelastic:** KE not conserved

sections.heading: Rotational Motion
sections.content:
**Arc length:** $s = r\theta$ \qquad **Tangential velocity:** $v = r\omega$

**Torque:** $\tau = rF\sin\theta = I\alpha$

**Rotational inertia (point mass):** $I = mr^2$

**Angular momentum:** $L = I\omega$ \qquad **Conservation:** $L_i = L_f$

$$\tau_{net} = \frac{\Delta L}{\Delta t}$$

sections.heading: Simple Harmonic Motion & Waves
sections.content:
**Period of a spring:** $T = 2\pi\sqrt{\dfrac{m}{k}}$

**Period of a pendulum:** $T = 2\pi\sqrt{\dfrac{l}{g}}$

**Wave speed:** $v = f\lambda$ \qquad $f = \dfrac{1}{T}$

**Intensity:** $I \propto A^2$ \quad **Doppler:** $f' = f\dfrac{v \pm v_o}{v \mp v_s}$

sections.heading: Electrostatics
sections.content:
**Coulomb's Law:** $F = k\dfrac{q_1 q_2}{r^2}$, \quad $k = 8.99 \times 10^9$ N·m²/C²

**Electric field:** $E = \dfrac{F}{q} = k\dfrac{Q}{r^2}$

**Electric potential:** $V = k\dfrac{Q}{r}$ \qquad $W = q\Delta V$

**Capacitance:** $C = \dfrac{Q}{V}$ \qquad $U_C = \tfrac{1}{2}CV^2$

**Parallel-plate capacitor:** $C = \dfrac{\varepsilon_0 A}{d}$, \quad $\varepsilon_0 = 8.85 \times 10^{-12}$ C²/N·m²

sections.heading: DC Circuits
sections.content:
$$V = IR \qquad P = IV = I^2R = \frac{V^2}{R}$$

**Series:** $R_{total} = R_1 + R_2 + \cdots$ \quad (same $I$)

**Parallel:** $\dfrac{1}{R_{total}} = \dfrac{1}{R_1} + \dfrac{1}{R_2} + \cdots$ \quad (same $V$)

**Kirchhoff's Voltage Law (KVL):** $\sum V = 0$ around any loop

**Kirchhoff's Current Law (KCL):** $\sum I_{in} = \sum I_{out}$

**RC time constant:** $\tau = RC$

sections.heading: Magnetism
sections.content:
**Magnetic force on charge:** $F = qvB\sin\theta$

**Magnetic force on wire:** $F = BIL\sin\theta$

**Magnetic flux:** $\Phi_B = BA\cos\theta$

**Faraday's Law:** $\mathcal{E} = -\dfrac{\Delta\Phi_B}{\Delta t}$

**Solenoid field:** $B = \mu_0 nI$, \quad $\mu_0 = 4\pi \times 10^{-7}$ T·m/A

sections.heading: Optics
sections.content:
**Snell's Law:** $n_1\sin\theta_1 = n_2\sin\theta_2$

**Index of refraction:** $n = \dfrac{c}{v}$, \quad $c = 3 \times 10^8$ m/s

**Thin lens equation:** $\dfrac{1}{f} = \dfrac{1}{d_o} + \dfrac{1}{d_i}$

**Magnification:** $m = -\dfrac{d_i}{d_o}$

Converging lens: $f > 0$; Diverging lens: $f < 0$

sections.heading: Thermodynamics
sections.content:
**First Law:** $\Delta U = Q - W$

**Ideal gas:** $PV = nRT$ \quad ($R = 8.314$ J/mol·K)

**Thermal expansion:** $\Delta L = \alpha L_0 \Delta T$

**Efficiency:** $e = \dfrac{W}{Q_H}$ \quad **Carnot efficiency:** $e_C = 1 - \dfrac{T_C}{T_H}$

referenceSheet.title: AP Physics C: Mechanics Formula Sheet

sections.heading: Kinematics (Calculus-Based)
sections.content:
$$v = \frac{dx}{dt} \qquad a = \frac{dv}{dt} = \frac{d^2x}{dt^2}$$

$$x = \int v\, dt \qquad v = \int a\, dt$$

sections.heading: Newton's Laws
sections.content:
$$\vec{F}_{net} = m\vec{a} = m\frac{d^2\vec{r}}{dt^2}$$

**Work:** $W = \int \vec{F} \cdot d\vec{r}$

**Work-Energy Theorem:** $W_{net} = \Delta KE$

**Power:** $P = \vec{F}\cdot\vec{v} = \dfrac{dW}{dt}$

sections.heading: Systems of Particles & Momentum
sections.content:
**Center of mass:** $\vec{r}_{cm} = \dfrac{\sum m_i \vec{r}_i}{M}$

$$\vec{p} = m\vec{v} \qquad \vec{F}_{net} = \frac{d\vec{p}}{dt}$$

**Impulse:** $\vec{J} = \int \vec{F}\, dt = \Delta\vec{p}$

sections.heading: Rotation
sections.content:
$$\tau_{net} = I\alpha = \frac{dL}{dt}$$

**Moment of inertia:** $I = \int r^2\, dm$

**Common values:** $I_{solid\ disk} = \tfrac{1}{2}MR^2$; $I_{rod\ (center)} = \tfrac{1}{12}ML^2$; $I_{sphere} = \tfrac{2}{5}MR^2$

**Parallel axis theorem:** $I = I_{cm} + Md^2$

**Angular momentum:** $L = I\omega \qquad \vec{L} = \vec{r}\times\vec{p}$

**Rotational KE:** $KE_{rot} = \tfrac{1}{2}I\omega^2$

sections.heading: Oscillations & Gravitation
sections.content:
**SHM:** $\dfrac{d^2x}{dt^2} = -\omega^2 x$ \quad $\omega = \sqrt{k/m}$ (spring), $\omega = \sqrt{g/l}$ (pendulum)

**Gravitational PE:** $U_g = -\dfrac{GMm}{r}$

**Orbital speed:** $v = \sqrt{\dfrac{GM}{r}}$ \qquad **Escape speed:** $v_e = \sqrt{\dfrac{2GM}{r}}$

**Kepler's 3rd Law:** $T^2 = \dfrac{4\pi^2}{GM}a^3$

referenceSheet.title: AP Physics C: E&M Formula Sheet

sections.heading: Electrostatics
sections.content:
**Coulomb's Law:** $\vec{F} = k\dfrac{q_1 q_2}{r^2}\hat{r}$

**Gauss's Law:** $\oint \vec{E}\cdot d\vec{A} = \dfrac{Q_{enc}}{\varepsilon_0}$

**Electric field from potential:** $\vec{E} = -\nabla V$

**Potential energy:** $U = k\dfrac{q_1 q_2}{r}$

sections.heading: Capacitance & Dielectrics
sections.content:
$$C = \frac{Q}{V} \qquad U_C = \frac{1}{2}CV^2 = \frac{Q^2}{2C}$$

**Parallel plate:** $C = \dfrac{\varepsilon_0 A}{d}$ (with dielectric: $C = \dfrac{\kappa\varepsilon_0 A}{d}$)

**Series:** $\dfrac{1}{C_{eq}} = \displaystyle\sum_i \dfrac{1}{C_i}$ \qquad **Parallel:** $C_{eq} = \displaystyle\sum_i C_i$

sections.heading: DC Circuits
sections.content:
**Ohm's Law:** $V = IR$ \qquad $P = IV$

**RC circuit charging:** $q(t) = C\mathcal{E}\left(1 - e^{-t/RC}\right)$

**RC time constant:** $\tau = RC$

**RL circuit:** $i(t) = \dfrac{\mathcal{E}}{R}\left(1 - e^{-Rt/L}\right)$, \quad $\tau = L/R$

**Inductor energy:** $U_L = \tfrac{1}{2}LI^2$

sections.heading: Magnetism
sections.content:
**Biot-Savart:** $d\vec{B} = \dfrac{\mu_0}{4\pi}\dfrac{I\,d\vec{l}\times\hat{r}}{r^2}$

**Ampere's Law:** $\oint \vec{B}\cdot d\vec{l} = \mu_0 I_{enc}$

**Lorentz force:** $\vec{F} = q(\vec{E} + \vec{v}\times\vec{B})$

**Faraday's Law:** $\mathcal{E} = -\dfrac{d\Phi_B}{dt}$

**Mutual inductance:** $\mathcal{E}_2 = -M\dfrac{dI_1}{dt}$

sections.heading: Population & Energy
sections.content:
**Population growth rate:**

$$r = (\text{birth rate} + \text{immigration rate}) - (\text{death rate} + \text{emigration rate})$$

**Rule of 10:** Only ~10% of energy transfers between trophic levels

$$\text{Ecological efficiency} = \frac{\text{energy at higher level}}{\text{energy at lower level}} \times 100\%$$

**IPAT equation:** $I = P \times A \times T$

$I$ = environmental impact, $P$ = population, $A$ = affluence, $T$ = technology

sections.heading: Water & Soil
sections.content:
**Permeability** — how easily water flows through material

**Porosity** — fraction of volume that is pore space

**Residence time:** $\text{residence time} = \dfrac{\text{reservoir size}}{\text{flow rate}}$

sections.heading: Atmospheric & Energy Calculations
sections.content:
**Parts per million (ppm):** $\dfrac{\text{mass of pollutant}}{\text{total mass}} \times 10^6$

**Energy use / power:**

$$\text{Power (W)} = \frac{\text{Energy (J)}}{\text{time (s)}}$$

**Percent efficiency:** $\eta = \dfrac{\text{useful energy out}}{\text{total energy in}} \times 100\%$

**Half-life:** $N(t) = N_0\left(\dfrac{1}{2}\right)^{t/t_{1/2}}$

sections.heading: Pollution & Risk
sections.content:
**LD50** — dose lethal to 50% of test population

**Risk** = probability of harm × magnitude of harm

**Dose-response relationship:** effect increases with dose (for most toxins)

**Bioaccumulation** — buildup in one organism; **Biomagnification** — increase up food chain

sections.heading: Descriptive Statistics
sections.content:
**Sample mean:** $\bar{x} = \dfrac{\sum x_i}{n}$

**Sample standard deviation:** $s_x = \sqrt{\dfrac{\sum(x_i - \bar{x})^2}{n - 1}}$

**IQR:** $IQR = Q_3 - Q_1$

**Outlier rule:** $< Q_1 - 1.5(IQR)$ or $> Q_3 + 1.5(IQR)$

sections.heading: Probability
sections.content:
**Addition rule:** $P(A \cup B) = P(A) + P(B) - P(A \cap B)$

**Multiplication rule:** $P(A \cap B) = P(A) \cdot P(B|A)$

**Independence:** $P(A \cap B) = P(A) \cdot P(B)$

**Conditional:** $P(A|B) = \dfrac{P(A \cap B)}{P(B)}$

**Expected value:** $\mu_X = \sum x_i P(x_i)$

**Variance:** $\sigma_X^2 = \sum(x_i - \mu)^2 P(x_i)$

sections.heading: Distributions
sections.content:
**Binomial:** $P(X = k) = \binom{n}{k}p^k(1-p)^{n-k}$

$\mu = np$, $\sigma = \sqrt{np(1-p)}$

**z-score:** $z = \dfrac{x - \mu}{\sigma}$

**Sampling distribution of $\bar{x}$:**

$$\mu_{\bar{x}} = \mu \qquad \sigma_{\bar{x}} = \frac{\sigma}{\sqrt{n}}$$

**Sampling distribution of $\hat{p}$:**

$$\mu_{\hat{p}} = p \qquad \sigma_{\hat{p}} = \sqrt{\frac{p(1-p)}{n}}$$

sections.heading: Confidence Intervals
sections.content:
**General form:** $\text{statistic} \pm z^* \cdot SE$ (or $t^*$)

**1-prop z-interval:** $\hat{p} \pm z^*\sqrt{\dfrac{\hat{p}(1-\hat{p})}{n}}$

**1-sample t-interval:** $\bar{x} \pm t^*\dfrac{s}{\sqrt{n}}$ \quad ($df = n-1$)

**2-sample t-interval:** $(\bar{x}_1 - \bar{x}_2) \pm t^*\sqrt{\dfrac{s_1^2}{n_1} + \dfrac{s_2^2}{n_2}}$

sections.heading: Significance Tests
sections.content:
**General:** $\text{test statistic} = \dfrac{\text{statistic} - \text{parameter}}{SE}$

**z-test for proportion:** $z = \dfrac{\hat{p} - p_0}{\sqrt{p_0(1-p_0)/n}}$

**t-test for mean:** $t = \dfrac{\bar{x} - \mu_0}{s/\sqrt{n}}$

**Chi-square (goodness of fit / independence):** $\chi^2 = \displaystyle\sum \dfrac{(O - E)^2}{E}$

**Regression t-test:** $t = \dfrac{b - 0}{SE_b}$

sections.heading: Linear Regression
sections.content:
$$\hat{y} = a + bx$$

$$b = r\frac{s_y}{s_x} \qquad a = \bar{y} - b\bar{x}$$

**Residual:** $e = y - \hat{y}$

**$r$** = correlation coefficient ($-1 \leq r \leq 1$)

**$r^2$** = coefficient of determination (% variation in $y$ explained by $x$)

sections.heading: Trigonometric Identities
sections.content:
**Pythagorean:**

$$\sin^2\theta + \cos^2\theta = 1$$
$$1 + \tan^2\theta = \sec^2\theta \qquad 1 + \cot^2\theta = \csc^2\theta$$

**Sum / Difference:**

$$\sin(A \pm B) = \sin A\cos B \pm \cos A\sin B$$
$$\cos(A \pm B) = \cos A\cos B \mp \sin A\sin B$$

**Double Angle:**

$$\sin 2\theta = 2\sin\theta\cos\theta$$
$$\cos 2\theta = \cos^2\theta - \sin^2\theta = 1 - 2\sin^2\theta = 2\cos^2\theta - 1$$

**Half Angle:**

$$\sin^2\theta = \frac{1 - \cos 2\theta}{2} \qquad \cos^2\theta = \frac{1 + \cos 2\theta}{2}$$

sections.heading: Logarithm & Exponential Properties
sections.content:
$$\log_b(xy) = \log_b x + \log_b y$$
$$\log_b\!\left(\frac{x}{y}\right) = \log_b x - \log_b y$$
$$\log_b(x^n) = n\log_b x$$
$$\log_b x = \frac{\ln x}{\ln b} \qquad b^x = e^{x\ln b}$$

**Exponential growth/decay:** $f(t) = A \cdot b^t = A \cdot e^{kt}$

sections.heading: Sequences & Series
sections.content:
**Arithmetic:** $a_n = a_1 + (n-1)d$ \qquad $S_n = \dfrac{n(a_1 + a_n)}{2}$

**Geometric:** $a_n = a_1 r^{n-1}$ \qquad $S_n = \dfrac{a_1(1 - r^n)}{1 - r}$

**Infinite geometric** ($|r| < 1$): $S = \dfrac{a_1}{1-r}$

**Binomial Theorem:** $(a+b)^n = \displaystyle\sum_{k=0}^n \binom{n}{k}a^{n-k}b^k$

referenceSheet.title: AP CS A — Java Quick Reference

sections.heading: Primitive Types
sections.content:
| Type | Size | Range |
|---|---|---|
| `int` | 32-bit | $-2^{31}$ to $2^{31}-1$ |
| `double` | 64-bit | ~$\pm 1.8 \times 10^{308}$ |
| `boolean` | — | `true` / `false` |
| `char` | 16-bit Unicode | `'A'` = 65 |

**Integer division truncates:** `7 / 2 == 3`

**Modulo:** `7 % 3 == 1`

**Integer overflow** wraps silently — use `long` if needed.

sections.heading: String Methods
sections.content:
```java
String s = "Hello";
s.length()           // 5
s.substring(1, 3)    // "el"  (start inclusive, end exclusive)
s.substring(2)       // "llo"
s.indexOf("ll")      // 2  (-1 if not found)
s.equals("Hello")   // true  (never use ==)
s.compareTo("World") // negative (H < W)
s.charAt(0)          // 'H'
```

sections.heading: Arrays & ArrayList
Control Flow
Classes & Inheritance
Math & Casting
sections.content:
```java
Math.abs(x)        // absolute value
Math.pow(x, y)     // x^y (returns double)
Math.sqrt(x)       // √x (returns double)
Math.random()      // [0.0, 1.0)
// Random int in [a, b]:
(int)(Math.random() * (b - a + 1)) + a

// Casting
(int) 3.9    // 3  (truncates, not rounds)
(double) 7 / 2  // 3.5
```
